title: sorbus umbellata var. cretica
intro: Sorbus umbellata var. cretica, also known as the American mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to eastern North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds found in its berries and leaves. The berries of Sorbus umbellata var. cretica have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to their rich content of vitamins, minerals, and flavonoids. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed in traditional medicine to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
